ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for vegetarian Maggi stir-fry; 'noodle dish' (FOODON_03302051) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Noodles, cooked) is the closest parent; no specific code exists for vegetarian instant noodle stir-fry in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this specific dish; SNOMED CT does not enumerate individual prepared noodle dishes at this level of specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 300 g (one plate), representing a typical hawker or restaurant portion of stir-fried vegetarian Maggi noodles served in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Fried noodles (vegetarian) — HPB Singapore Foodo / Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo lists fried noodles (vegetarian) as the closest matching entry. Maggi instant noodles are a wheat-based instant noodle product; the HPB vegetarian fried noodle profile is used as the primary reference and cross-checked against USDA SR Legacy data for instant noodle stir-fry compositions. Values represent a cooked, stir-fried dish per 100 g edible portion.

gi_source.title: Noodles, instant, cooked — Glycemic Index Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for vegetarian Maggi stir-fry. The University of Sydney GI Database reports a GI of approximately 47 (low) for cooked instant noodles (wheat-based). Maggi noodles are wheat-based instant noodles; this value is used as the closest defensible analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (47 × 63.0) / 100 ≈ 29.6, where carbohydrate per 300 g serving = 21.0 × 3 = 63.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~1440 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size or request reduced-sodium preparation, as a single 300 g serving provides approximately 1440 mg sodium — nearly 60% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (GL ~29.6 per serving) from wheat-based instant noodles
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that a full 300 g serving carries a moderate-to-high glycemic load of approximately 29.6; portion control to half a plate and pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables can help moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate fat content; low saturated fat relative to total fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high sodium content (~1440 mg per serving) is the primary cardiovascular concern; saturated fat is relatively low at 1.1 g per 100 g, and the dish is cholesterol-free in its vegetarian formulation, making fat quality acceptable but sodium management critical for cardiovascular patients.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Vegetarian Maggi Noodle Stir-Fry (Mi Goi Xao Chay) is a popular hawker and casual-dining dish in Singapore, commonly found in Vietnamese-influenced eateries and multicultural food courts. The dish is prepared by briefly softening Maggi brand instant wheat noodles, then stir-frying them in a hot wok with vegetable oil, garlic, soy sauce, and an assortment of fresh vegetables such as cabbage, carrot, bean sprouts, and spring onion. The result is a savoury, slightly chewy noodle dish with a golden-brown colour, umami-forward flavour from soy sauce and Maggi seasoning, and a satisfying mix of textures from the tender noodles and crisp-tender vegetables. The dish is vegetarian and typically halal-certified in Singapore hawker settings. Nutritionally, it provides moderate energy (~148 kcal per 100 g), a reasonable protein contribution from the noodles and vegetables (~4.5 g per 100 g), and a low-to-moderate fat profile (~5.2 g per 100 g). The primary clinical concern is its sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g), which accumulates significantly in a full serving. The glycemic index is estimated at 47 (low) based on the wheat-based instant noodle base, though the glycemic load per full serving is moderate at approximately 29.6. This dish is suitable for general consumption but requires sodium and portion management for individuals with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease.
common_user_queries: What is Mi Goi Xao Chay?
How many calories in vegetarian Maggi stir-fry?
Is Maggi noodle stir-fry suitable for diabetics?
What is the sodium content of vegetarian Maggi noodles?
Is Mi Goi Xao Chay halal?
What vegetables are in vegetarian Maggi noodle?

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Foodo entry exists for Vegetarian Maggi Noodle Stir-Fry specifically; values are adapted from the HPB Singapore vegetarian fried noodle profile and cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy instant noodle data. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for cooked instant noodles. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the adapted (not exact) source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo 'fried noodles (vegetarian)' and cross-checked against USDA SR Legacy instant noodle stir-fry data; no exact branded Maggi vegetarian stir-fry entry was found in any approved primary source. 2. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo vegetarian fried noodle source entry. 3. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entry for this dish category. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entry; this dish contains no significant vitamin D source. 5. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entry. 6. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entry. 7. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 as this is a vegetarian dish with no animal-derived vitamin B12 sources; confirmed zero by formulation. 8. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as this is a plant-based dish with no cholesterol-containing ingredients; confirmed zero by formulation. 9. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as vegetable oils used in stir-frying at hawker level are not hydrogenated; confirmed negligible/zero per HPB source. 10. GI estimated at 47 (low) from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for cooked instant wheat noodles; no direct GI measurement for this specific dish was found. 11. The dish name 'Mi Goi Xao Chay' reflects Vietnamese-origin naming common in Singapore Vietnamese eateries; 'mi goi' = instant noodle packet, 'xao' = stir-fry, 'chay' = vegetarian. 12. Serving size of 300 g is an estimate based on typical Singapore hawker plate portions for noodle dishes.
